Dashboard Tiered History Cache

The Python operations dashboard uses an RRD-style cache for chart history so UI requests read bounded chart-ready data instead of rescanning long append logs.

Design

Raw JSONL histories remain the recovery ledger. Dashboard chart APIs read the tier cache. The hot tier is written to a RAM-backed path when available; older summaries are persisted on disk.
TierStorageAge RangeStepPurpose
minute/dev/shm or configured RAM dirlast 1 hour60 secondsSnappy live dashboard plots.
hourops/runtime/dashboard-history1 hour to 24 hours1 hourOne-day context without minute-level duplication.
dayops/runtime/dashboard-history24 hours to 7 days1 dayWeek-level trend context.
weekops/runtime/dashboard-history7 days to 31 days1 weekMonth-level trend context.

Implementation Notes

The cache stores each sample in exactly one age tier, selecting the latest sample in each bucket. This follows the same operating principle as RRDtool round-robin archives: keep bounded high-resolution recent data and lower-resolution older data.

New sampler writes update the tier cache incrementally. On dashboard startup or first use after the RAM tier disappeared, the cache is rebuilt from the append-only source log.

Prometheus storage and recording-rule documentation support the same general principle: keep incoming samples in a hot head/WAL path, compact persistent blocks, and precompute dashboard-friendly series for repeated dashboard reads.

Configuration

VariableDefault
BDAG_DASHBOARD_HISTORY_RAM_DIRAuto: /dev/shm/<project>-dashboard-history when writable.
BDAG_DASHBOARD_HISTORY_DISK_DIRops/runtime/dashboard-history
BDAG_DASHBOARD_HISTORY_HOT_SECONDS3600
BDAG_DASHBOARD_HISTORY_HOT_STEP_SECONDS60
BDAG_DASHBOARD_HISTORY_HOURLY_SECONDS86400
BDAG_DASHBOARD_HISTORY_DAILY_SECONDS604800
BDAG_DASHBOARD_HISTORY_WEEKLY_SECONDS2678400
Do not replace this with full-history reads in request paths. If the raw ledger grows, optimize rebuilds or add bounded source-tail indexing rather than returning to per-request scans.

References

  • RRDtool documentation: consolidation and bounded round-robin archives.
  • Prometheus storage documentation: hot head/WAL plus compacted persistent blocks and retention limits.
  • Prometheus recording rules: precompute frequently needed dashboard series.
